notes: Twenty-third validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school strives to create bilingual students with strong academic and intercultural competence as an international language public school.
Official materials say the school aims to be a national leader in academic achievement and language immersion education.
The school says its language immersion program begins in pre-kindergarten and focuses heavily on Mandarin Chinese instruction.
Official materials say students gain exposure to the world's three most spoken languages.
Official pages list Renee Mathews as chief executive officer and principal.
Its board page says trustees meet on the fourth Tuesday of each month at 6:30 p.m.
Its mission says the school engages students by integrating a challenging, hands-on academic program with multi-faceted fine arts courses in a familial environment.
Official materials say the school opened on July 28, 2008.
The school says it is guided by Aristotle's philosophy, "We Learn by Doing."
Official materials highlight Pride Scholar Pathways for middle school students in focused areas such as theatre arts, music arts, visual arts, and healthful living.
Official team pages list Michael Cox as executive director and Nancy Garcia as co-principal.
Official board pages list Dustin Embrey as board chair.
Its mission says the school empowers scholars to sharpen academic knowledge, demonstrate creative expression, and expand leadership abilities.
Official materials say the school emphasizes the growth of the whole child through A.C.E.: Academics, Community, and Enrichment.
The school says it incorporates the Paideia philosophy of teaching.
Official materials say the school partnered with Houghton Mifflin Harcourt for a comprehensive curriculum.
An expansion announcement tied the opening of a new elementary building to the school's 25th anniversary.
Official pages list Tonya Jackson as executive director and Adrienne Davis as board president.

schools.officialName: Cardinal Charter Acad at Wendell Falls

clues.text: Its mission says the school builds a student-centered, data-driven learning environment where students develop critical thinking skills and reach their academic potential.
Official materials say the school is part of the Charter Schools USA family.
The school says its model is built on collaboration, differentiation, and Future Ready skills.
Official materials highlight an integrated character education program and personalized learning plans.
Official news on the homepage says Barry White Jr. was selected as principal.
Its board pages say governance is handled by the Triangle Charter Education Association, the founding board for the school.

Its mission says the school, in partnership with family and community, enhances students' quality of life by ensuring they possess competencies for academic and vocational success in a technological world.
Official materials say the school collaborates with East Carolina University and other post-secondary institutions.
The school's homepage says it earned silver in the Daily Reflector's 2025 Best of Charter Schools category.
Official materials repeatedly frame the campus with the phrase, "Every day is a great day to be at Bear Grass Charter School!"
Official pages list Patricia Speller as principal.
Official board pages list Delmas B. Cumbee Jr. as chairman.
